document.title: Non-clinical Report For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Non-clinical report for the HAD1717MC X-ray Detector supporting FDA 510(k) submission testing.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides non-clinical testing data in conformance with FDA guidance for 510(k) submissions for solid-state X-ray imaging devices, including MTF and DQE measurements tested by IEC 62220-1.
keyPoints: Device is identified as X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, Report No. A-PR-0-001 (Rev.0).
Non-clinical testing includes physical characteristics, operational functions, DQE, MTF, aliasing, lag, pixel defects, recovery time, and exposure characteristics.
The device is described as a static X-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
The active area is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm and no under-scanning is possible.
Image data are transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
The HAD1717MC meets regulatory and IEC standards as outlined in referenced safety and EMC reports.

evidence.claim: The document is a non-clinical report for the HAD1717MC X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-clinical Report For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-PR-0-001 (Rev.0).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-PR-0-001 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision history identifies Rev. 0 dated Sep/20/2019 as establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Sep/20/2019 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The report was prepared by R&D team member Kim, Ki Hyun on Sep/20/2019 and approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu on Sep/21/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Kim, Ki Hyun Sep/20/2019 ... Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Sep/21/2019

evidence.claim: The non-clinical testing data were provided in conformance with FDA 510(k) guidance for solid-state X-ray imaging devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-clinical testing data was provided in conformance to the FDA “Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)’s for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices”
evidence.pageTarget: FDA 510(k) Guidance for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices

evidence.claim: The report references IEC 62220-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for DQE determination for radiographic imaging detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62220-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector overall dimensions including outer short ring are 470 mm by 470 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Entire dimensions include outer short ring 470 (X) mm x 470 (Y) mm

evidence.claim: The panel size with outer short ring removed is 445 mm by 445 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Panel size (outer short ring removed) 445 mm x 445 mm

evidence.claim: The active pixel area size is 427.28 mm by 427.28 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm

evidence.claim: The active area pixel count is 3052 by 3052, totaling 9,314,704 pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 3052 x 3052 = 9,314,704

evidence.claim: The device frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate 1 fps

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The X-ray absorber or scintillator is commercially available and converts X-ray energy to light.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray absorber(scintillator) is commercially available and converts x-ray energy directly to light.

evidence.claim: The report identifies CsI and Gadox as two scintillator types.
evidence.evidenceText: There are two types of scintillator which are CsI and Gadox.

evidence.claim: The absorber wavelength is 540 nm in the visible region.
evidence.evidenceText: This absorbers wavelength is 540nm(Visible region).

evidence.claim: Energy conversion is described as X-ray to visible light, then analog-to-digital conversion.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray Visible light (450nm ~ 550nm) Object Analog to Digital Conversion

evidence.claim: Output image data are transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 16-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC DQE criteria include at least 45% at 0.1 lp/mm and at least 35% at 0.5 lp/mm.
Measured HAD1717MC DQE values include 47.8% at 0.1 lp/mm, 39.8% at 0.5 lp/mm, 31.9% at 1.0 lp/mm, 23.5% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 18.9% at 3.0 lp/mm.
The DQE measurement procedure used IEC 62220-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The measurement procedure described in the report IEC 62220-1 was used

evidence.claim: The detector pitch is 0.14 mm with a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.

evidence.claim: The report states artifacts due to aliasing will be negligible.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible.

evidence.claim: Lag measurement actual result is 0.000171.
evidence.evidenceText: Lag = ((a-b)-(c-d)) / ((b+d)/2) / Actual measurements : 0.000171

evidence.claim: The active area is fully used and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 427.28 mm x 427.28 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The X-ray system is designed for detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.

evidence.claim: Pixel defect criteria include Class #0 at no more than 2,400 pixels and Class #7 at no more than 0 pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: Defect Class #0 : ≤ 2,400 Pixel ... Defect Class #7 : ≤ 0 Pixel

evidence.claim: HAD1717MC measured pixel defect results include Class #0: 6 pixels, Class #1: 1 pixel, and defect density: 0 pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC : Defect Class #0 : 6 Pixel Defect Class #1 : 1 Pixel ... Defect Density : 0 Pixel

evidence.claim: After X-ray exposure, detector readout requires at least 2 seconds before the detector is ready for the next exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 2 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure.

evidence.claim: Image readout, transfer, and pre-processing require under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: Device stability time is 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: - Stability time : 5 sec.

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC meets regulatory standards outlined in safety report DRMCBL1909-0016 and EMC report DREKCEE1905-0342.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: The HAD1717MC meets IEC standards outlined in the referenced safety and EMC reports.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1717MC meets the IEC standards as outlined in the HAD1717MC safety report: DRMCBL1909-0016, EMC report: DREKCEE1905-0342

evidence.claim: The reported exposure condition for the HAD1717MC line chart is RQA 5 at 70 kV, 200 mA, SID 150 cm, Al 21 mm, and 200 ms.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposure Condition - RQA 5 : 70kV, 200mA, SID : 150cm, Al 21mm - 200ms

evidence.claim: Comparative MTF results list HAD1717MC at 94.90% at 0.1 lp, 82.60% at 0.5 lp, 65.60% at 1 lp, 36.10% at 2 lp, and 21.50% at 3 lp.
Comparative DQE results list HAD1717MC at 70.10% at 0.1 lp, 57.40% at 0.5 lp, 52.10% at 1 lp, 38% at 2 lp, and 28.40% at 3 lp.

reviewFlags.reason: Some equations and figures are missing or reduced to placeholders in the source text, including DQE formula, quantum-noise model, cross-section diagrams, and plotted characteristics.
Authority is inferred from FDA guidance and source metadata; the document itself appears to be an internal non-clinical report prepared, reviewed, and approved by company personnel.
No FDA 510(k) number, FDA decision date, manufacturer legal name, or final regulatory clearance status is present in the extracted source text.
The source contains OCR or conversion quality issues, including typographical errors and malformed values such as '3..0 lp/mm' and missing mathematical symbols.
